doc: Mistral custom structured outputs (response_format json_schema, strict:true) schema-node conventions; see https://docs.mistral.ai/capabilities/structured-output/custom_structured_output/ and the examples it links to. THIN pack: Mistral's docs do NOT enumerate a per-keyword 'unsupported features' list, so this pack adds NO keyword-blocklist rules (inventing one would violate primary-sourcing). The two rules below are EXAMPLE-DERIVED: Mistral's docs build strict structured outputs from json_schema definitions (the docs' Pydantic-model examples serialize to objects that set additionalProperties:false and list every property in 'required'). The docs do NOT state these as a prose MUST — they are observed conventions of the strict json_schema path, not documented hard rejections. API/model-level notes that are NOT lint rules: the request must use response_format json_schema with strict:true, and all models except codestral-mamba are supported.

rules.reason: Mistral's custom structured-output example sets additionalProperties:false on each object (example-derived; the docs show but do not prose-mandate it).
Mistral's custom structured-output example lists every defined property in 'required' (example-derived; the docs show but do not prose-mandate it).
